title: FC Dynamo-2 Kyiv

for at least four seasons yet, to differentiate it from the team of masters, it was referred to as Dynamo-klubnaya.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football club

FC Dynamo-2 Kyiv

**FC Dynamo-2 Kyiv** was the second [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") team of the [Ukrainian](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine") [football club](/wiki/Football_club "Football club") [Dynamo Kyiv](/wiki/Dynamo_Kyiv "Dynamo Kyiv") based in [Kyiv](/wiki/Kyiv "Kyiv"), [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"). The team was first created in 1938, and the club ceased its recent operations again after the 2015–16 season.[[1]](#cite_note-DK2-1) During the Soviet period, participation of the second squad was sporadic and mostly at republican level, the [Football Championship of the Ukrainian SSR](/wiki/Football_Championship_of_the_Ukrainian_SSR "Football Championship of the Ukrainian SSR").

During the first couple of decades of the Ukrainian second tier (Persha Liha), the team dominated the league competition and won three titles. Later its performance declined, and the club concentrated its effort on its junior squads competing in parallel competitions for the youth.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Dynamo-2_Kyiv&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

See also: [FC Dynamo Kyiv](/wiki/FC_Dynamo_Kyiv "FC Dynamo Kyiv")

The team existed as early as 1938 when it first played at the top tier of the Ukrainian Football Championship placing 8th among 12 teams. Before the [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") the [Ukrainian Football Championship](/wiki/Football_Championship_of_the_Ukrainian_SSR "Football Championship of the Ukrainian SSR") was a tier-structured republican level competition two levels below the 2 tiers All-Union Football League.

Following the [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), Dynamo Kyiv continued to field its second squad in the [Football Championship of the Ukrainian SSR](/wiki/Football_Championship_of_the_Ukrainian_SSR "Football Championship of the Ukrainian SSR"), but under name **Dynamo Kyiv** for at least four seasons yet, to differentiate it from the team of masters, it was referred to as Dynamo-klubnaya.

In 1965, there was created the second team of [FC Dynamo Kyiv](/wiki/FC_Dynamo_Kyiv "FC Dynamo Kyiv") and replaced FC Temp Kyiv (that represented the [Kyiv Aircraft Factory](/wiki/Kyiv_Aircraft_Factory "Kyiv Aircraft Factory")). It only competed for one season in the Ukrainian Class B football competitions of the [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union").

In 1992, Dynamo-2 Kyiv was revived based on its double (reserve team, Dynamo-d Kyiv) which played in competition for reserves teams of the [Soviet Top League](/wiki/Soviet_Top_League "Soviet Top League"). The new team was admitted to the [1992 Ukrainian First League](/wiki/1992_Ukrainian_First_League "1992 Ukrainian First League").

In 2004, when there was created a separate competition for reserve teams of the [Ukrainian Premier League](/wiki/Ukrainian_Premier_League "Ukrainian Premier League"), the team was preserved and continued to compete at a professional level in regular league competitions until 2016.

The team participated regularly in the [Ukrainian First League](/wiki/Ukrainian_First_League "Ukrainian First League"), since it cannot be promoted to the [Ukrainian Premier League](/wiki/Ukrainian_Premier_League "Ukrainian Premier League"), being a reserve team from the [FC Dynamo Kyiv](/wiki/FC_Dynamo_Kyiv "FC Dynamo Kyiv") franchise. Like most tributary teams, the best players are sent up to the senior team, meanwhile developing other players for further call-ups.

## Honors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Dynamo-2_Kyiv&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Honors")]

* **[Ukrainian First League](/wiki/Ukrainian_First_League "Ukrainian First League"):**
  + **Champions (3):** 1998–99, 1999–00, 2000–01
  + **Runners Up (2):** 1996–97, 1997–98

Oleksandr Ryzhenko. [Плей-офф первой лиги. "Динамо-2" – "Шахтер" 1:0. Когда даже поражение оставляет шансы](https://web.archive.org/web/20130619021952/http://www.ua-football.com/ukrainian/first/51bdf1d4.html) [Play-off First League. Dynamo-2 – Shakhtar 1:0. When even the defeat leaves the odds.] (in Russian). ua-football.com. Archived from [the original](http://www.ua-football.com/ukrainian/first/51bdf1d4.html) on 19 June 2013. Retrieved 16 June 2013.
